---
name: Security advisory (private)
description: Privately report a security vulnerability. Use this *only* if email to security@msg-gateway.example.com is unavailable.
title: "[security]: "
labels: ["security", "triage"]
assignees: ["@msg-gateway/security"]
---

<!--
This template mirrors the disclosure flow described in SECURITY.md.
If you can, prefer emailing `security@msg-gateway.example.com` or
opening a *private* advisory through the *Security → Advisories*
tab on GitHub. Filing a public issue for a vulnerability is a
disclosure; do not use this template unless both channels above
are unavailable.
-->

## Vulnerability summary

<!-- One-sentence description; the maintainer will not open the diff yet. -->

## Impact

<!--
Who is affected, what data can be exposed, what is the worst-case
outcome? Pick the closest severity from SECURITY.md.
-->

- **Severity**: <S0 outage | S1 data leak | S2 privilege escalation | S3 informational>

## Affected versions / commit SHAs

<!-- Range or list; include the SHA you tested against. -->

## Reproduction

<!--
A proof of concept. Host the artefacts privately (private repo,
gist with `secret` flag, or a one-time download link) and paste
the URL here. **Do not** paste exploits into a public issue.
-->

## Suggested fix (optional)

<!-- If you have a suggestion, link to a private patch or describe the change. -->

## Disclosure timeline

<!--
What is the disclosure window you are willing to follow? We default
to 90 days; if you need a different timeline, mention it here.
-->

## Contact

<!-- How can the team reach you (email, GitHub handle, GPG key fingerprint)? -->
